visualGoal: A settings how-to / explainer: the load-bearing visuals are DIAGRAMS (what clipping does to the force signal, the diagnose-the-dead-feel decision tree, the 5% walk-down loop, per-sim meter locations) and OWNED meter screenshots — NOT product photos. One verified product photo (MOZA R12) anchors the narrow 'more headroom' edge case. Do NOT force unrelated verified hardware into an FFB explainer to inflate image count.

infographicPrompts.title: What clipping does to the force signal
content: Requested force wave rising past the motor ceiling (e.g. 12Nm line)
Lost cues labeled: oversteer onset, front-grip loss, curb strike
Same wave at lower gain: full detail preserved under the ceiling
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, waveform + horizontal ceiling line, racing colors, large readable labels

infographicPrompts.title: Is it actually clipping? Diagnose the 'dead' feel
content: Strong but flat wall under load, worst on curbs -> CLIPPING (lower gain)
Weak/vague everywhere -> too little gain or low-torque base (raise gain)
Notchy/rattly on straights, fine under load -> cogging / Min Force / over-filter (set Min Force 0)
FFB vanished mid-session on MOZA -> Hands-Off Protection (check Pit House)
infographicPrompts.style: Four-branch decision tree, colour-coded, product-photo-free, big text; mirrors the article's decision-tree callout

infographicPrompts.title: The 5% walk-down loop
content: 2 Drive 5 laps on a GT3 with one big curb
3 Pinned at top through normal corners? -5% gain
5 Stop when it flashes at the top only on genuine impacts
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered flow loop, product-photo-free, big text

infographicPrompts.title: Where the force meter lives, sim by sim
content: iRacing: press F -> F bar fills orange = clipping
ACC: no native meter -> FFBClip app or SimHub overlay, or feel for the wall
LMU: no numeric meter -> set torque cap + feel, or SimHub overlay
Any sim: SimHub community FFB overlay
infographicPrompts.style: Four-panel cheat sheet, real OWNED screenshots preferred where available; NO fabricated game UI

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez at a direct-drive rig, one hand easing an in-game gain slider down, force-meter overlay visible on the monitor (real owned capture, not fabricated UI), confident-mechanic energy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded: OWNED capture of iRacing F-bar clipping (bar full and orange) vs a healthy breathing bar
OWNED capture of the ACC FFBClip app graph: delivered-force trace flatlined against the ceiling vs spiking-but-breathing
OWNED capture of a SimHub FFB-meter overlay in LMU / rFactor 2 (bar pinned at top vs moving)
NEVER AI-generate fake game UI or telemetry for any of these.
openQuestions: Re-verify iRacing native-360Hz 'irFFB not required for DD' claim at cert time (season/firmware-dependent).
Confirm a stable link for the ACC FFBClip app before hyperlinking it (OverTake download page 403s to automated fetch); currently named-not-linked in the body.
Confirm SimHub still has no single built-in one-click clip warning (capability is community FFB-meter dashboards + irFFB2026 overlay).

This how-to legitimately cannot reach 8 verified product images without forcing irrelevant hardware; certification should rest on the four infographics + OWNED meter screenshots above, not product-photo count.
